subject: Physics
title: Planck's Desperate Trick
description: A micro-lesson that makes Planck's Desperate Trick usable.

hook.text: In 1900, Max Planck solved a physics crisis by proposing an idea he didn't even believe himself.

buildup.text: Classical physics predicted hot objects emit infinite energy at short wavelengths. They obviously don't.

discovery.text: Planck assumed energy comes in tiny packets—quanta—not a continuous flow. The math suddenly worked.

twist.text: Planck considered it a mathematical trick. He didn't realize he'd just launched a revolution.

climax.text: His constant, h, became the foundation of quantum mechanics—the most successful theory in physics.

punchline.text: A desperate trick. The most accurate theory ever. Accidents happen.

quiz.question: What radical assumption did Planck make to solve the ultraviolet catastrophe?
options: Energy comes in discrete packets called quanta
Heat cannot be transferred through empty space
